# UC-22.2.29 ┬и NIS2 Cryptography Policy Compliance Рђћ TLS and Certificate Posture

## Description

Correlates passive TLS observations from Stream with inventory-derived certificate expirations and `nis2_scoped_service_map` to list only NIS2-scoped listeners that run outdated protocols or face thirty-day renewals.

## Value

Weak TLS versions are invisible until a pen test or outage; combining wire-derived protocol data with scoped service context lets risk owners prioritize renewals that actually map to regulated workloads instead of every lab VM in the enterprise.

## Implementation

(1) Mirror production VLANs to Stream. (2) Maintain `nis2_scoped_service_map`. (3) Import `nis2_crypto_policy.csv`. (4) Alert on `weak_tls=1`. (5) PKI workflow webhook on `cert_problem=1`. (6) Annual evidence export + `evidenceSigning`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
Рђб Splunk Stream Independent Stream Forwarder РЅЦ8.x with **Configure Рєњ Stream Рєњ Capture** selecting TLS handshakes only (disable payload retention).
Рђб Network SPAN/RSPAN capacity validated (rule of thumb: 3РђЊ5% of total bandwidth).
Рђб Splunk Add-on for Stream ([Splunkbase 1809](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809)) on Stream Forwarder + Search Head for field extractions.
Рђб CSV `nis2_scoped_service_map` listing `dest_ip,service_name,nis2_in_scope` approved by legal weekly.
Рђб Splunk Enterprise Security optional for correlation with `assets_by_cidr` if internal IP overlaps.

Step 1 Рђћ Configure data collection
Рђб On Stream Forwarder `/opt/streamfwd/local/streamfwd.conf` set `port_capture = 443,8443,6443` and `protocol = tls`.
Рђб Confirm Splunk receives `index=pki` `sourcetype=stream:tls` within 2 minutes via `tcpdump` + HEC ack.
Рђб Maintain `nis2_crypto_policy.csv` with columns `service_family,min_tls_version,allowed_cipher_suites`.
Рђб Import certificate inventory nightly to `index=certificates` for reconciliation.

Step 2 Рђћ Create the search and alert
Use primary `spl`; add companion lookup:
```spl
| lookup nis2_crypto_policy service_name OUTPUT min_tls_version allowed_ciphers
| eval policy_break=if(mvfind(split(allowed_ciphers,","),cipher)=-1,1,0)
```
Schedule alert hourly if `weak_tls=1`.

Step 3 Рђћ Validate
Рђб Pick VIP: compare Splunk `tls_version` to `openssl s_client -connect vip:443 -tls1_2`.
Рђб Validate `cert_expiry` vs `x509` PEM from PKI vault.
Рђб For scoped map, ping IPAM **Infoblox** / **BlueCat** owner to ensure IP still assigned to service.

Step 4 Рђћ Operationalize
Рђб Dashboard with two tabs: **Protocol downgrade**, **Renewal clock**.
Рђб ServiceNow change requests auto-opened with `correlation_id = dest_ip + dest_port`.
Рђб Signed monthly PDF referencing `evidenceSigning` TSA receipt.

Step 5 Рђћ Troubleshooting
Рђб **No dest_ip** Рђћ NAT metadata missing; enable `add_field(dest_ip)` in Stream pipeline.
Рђб **`cert_expiry` multivalue** Рђћ use `mvindex(cert_expiry,-1)` after stats.
Рђб **High false weak_tls** Рђћ middle boxes strip TLS extensions; verify with packet capture on SPAN.
Рђб **Lookup missing** Рђћ `nis2_scoped_service_map` outdated after cloud migration; refresh via Terraform output.


## SPL

```spl
index=pki sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(tls_version) as tls_version latest(cipher) as cipher values(expiry_epoch) as cert_expiry by dest_ip dest_port
| lookup nis2_scoped_service_map dest_ip OUTPUT service_name nis2_in_scope
| where nis2_in_scope="true"
| eval weak_tls=if(match(tls_version,"SSLv|TLSv1\.0|TLSv1\.1"),1,0)
| eval cert_problem=if(cert_expiry < relative_time(now(),"+30d@d"),1,0)
| where weak_tls=1 OR cert_problem=1
| table service_name, dest_ip, dest_port, tls_version, cipher, cert_problem
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t min(All_Certificates.ssl_end_time) as soonest from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates by All_Certificates.ssl_subject | where soonest < relative_time(now(),"+30d")
```

## Visualization

Table with color rules on tls_version, timeline for cert_expiry, map of dest_ip.
